from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent, natural‑language sentence.
    """
    # Mapping from predicate to a generic phrase.
    verb_map = {
        "architecturalStyle": "has an architectural style of",
        "buildingStartDate": "was started in",
        "completionDate": "was completed in",
        "floorCount": "has",
        "location": "is located in",
        "cost": "costs",
        "floorArea": "covers a floor area of",
        "architect": "was designed by",
        "owner": "is owned by",
        "formerName": "was formerly known as",
        "height": "rises to",
        "buildingType": "is a",
        "developer": "was developed by",
        "tenant": "has a tenant",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "country": "is in",
        "currentTenants": "currently houses",
        "address": "has the address",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "bedCount": "has",
        "region": "is in the region of",
        "state": "is in the state of",
        "website": "has website",
        "yearOfConstruction": "was constructed in",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "has NRHP reference number",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "was added to the NRHP on",
        "significantBuilding": "is known for the building",
        "governingBody": "is governed by",
        "leader": "is led by",
        "chancellor": "has chancellor",
        "governmentType": "has government type",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "language": "is spoken in",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "currency": "has currency",
        "ethnicGroup": "has an ethnic group",
        "origin": "originates from",
        "significantProject": "is known for the project",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in",
        "demonym": "has demonym",
        "numberOfRooms": "has",
        "keyPerson": "has key person",
        "architecture": "features architecture",
        "postalCode": "has postcode area",
    }

    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_groups[t.subject].append(t)

    def clean(val: str) -> str:
        return val.strip().strip('"').strip("'")

    sentences = []
    for subject, group in subject_groups.items():
        clauses = []

        # First handle construction dates together for smoother phrasing
        start = next((clean(t.object) for t in group if t.predicate == "buildingStartDate"), None)
        end = next((clean(t.object) for t in group if t.predicate == "completionDate"), None)
        if start:
            if end:
                clauses.append(f"Construction of {subject} began in {start} and was completed in {end}")
                # mark handled
                handled_preds = {"buildingStartDate", "completionDate"}
            else:
                clauses.append(f"Construction of {subject} began in {start}")
                handled_preds = {"buildingStartDate"}
        else:
            handled_preds = set()

        # Then handle special predicates that need a specific template
        special_templates = {
            "leader": lambda o: f"The leader of {subject} is {o}",
            "capital": lambda o: f"The capital of {subject} is {o}",
            "language": lambda o: f"{o} is spoken in {subject}",
            "demonym": lambda o: f"The demonym for {subject} is {o}",
            "postalCode": lambda o: f"The postcode area of {subject} is {o

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
